articles.title: Eulogy to Emmeline B. Wells (Sonnet)
articles.author: O. F. Ursenbach
articles.complete_text:
How I recall, in those fond erstwhile days,
A sweet kind face, my dear old mother's friend;
Though but a lad I, when she won my praise,
To see her muse, blessed life with nature blend,
A child with children, friend to the oppressed,
Yet honored by the mightiest of men;
Few souls with like congenial powers are blessed.
To deal with kings, then lowly life again.
Now, snows of many winters blanche her brow;
Though ninety-two long years of mortal strife
Have aged her frame, her spirit, even now,
Presents each day a more majestic life.
May her declining years be decked with peace,
And may her soul live on and never cease.
articles.notes: This is a complete sonnet poem that appears to be separate from the main article content, though it shares the same title as an existing article. This appears to be the actual poem content while the existing file may be prose.
